title: Irregular Pluralization
description: Master the Latin and Greek plural forms of high-frequency academic nouns to eliminate common errors in scholarly writing.
lesson_content_markdown:
# Irregular Pluralization

## Academic Vocabulary and Latin/Greek Plurals

Academic English has inherited a large number of nouns from Latin and Greek. These nouns follow irregular plural patterns that differ from the standard English **-s/-es** suffix. Misusing these forms is one of the most conspicuous errors in formal academic writing.

---

## High-Frequency Academic Irregular Plurals

| Singular | Plural | Field of Use |
|---|---|---|
| **datum** | **data** | Research, Statistics |
| **criterion** | **criteria** | Evaluation, Methodology |
| **phenomenon** | **phenomena** | Science, Social Science |
| **hypothesis** | **hypotheses** | Research |
| **thesis** | **theses** | Academia |
| **analysis** | **analyses** | Statistics, Research |
| **curriculum** | **curricula** | Education |
| **medium** | **media** | Communication |
| **index** | **indices** | Mathematics, Economics |
| **appendix** | **appendices** | Academic Writing |
| **alumnus/alumna** | **alumni/alumnae** | Higher Education |
| **symposium** | **symposia** | Academia |

---

## The Most Common Errors

❌ *three criterias* → ✅ *three **criteria***
❌ *two phenomenons* → ✅ *two **phenomena***
❌ *the hypothesises* → ✅ *the **hypotheses***
❌ *all curriculums* → ✅ *all **curricula***

---

## Academic Writing Tip

Note that **criteria** and **data** are already plural. Avoid writing *a criteria* or *this criteria* — the correct singular forms are **a criterion** and **a datum** (or *a data point*).

mastery_check.prompt_to_student: The passage below contains five irregular pluralization errors. Identify each incorrect word and type the correct academic plural form.

activity_data.sentence: The study examined three phenomenons that are common in multilingual classrooms. Each criterias was evaluated independently by two trained assessors. The collected datas were subsequently entered into a statistical software program. The board reviewed the thesises submitted by the doctoral candidates. The curriculums of all participating institutions were compared to identify common patterns in language instruction.
